steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence already has a main verb — "shows" ("Aslanian's macroanalysis ___ nearly 1,000 book titles... shows not only... but also..."). The blank modifies "macroanalysis" and must not create a second competing main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘had examined’: A finite past-perfect verb. This would create two main verbs in one clause ("had examined... shows"), which is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘examining’: A present participle. It modifies "macroanalysis" as a participial phrase ("macroanalysis examining nearly 1,000 book titles") without competing with "shows." ✓
  • Choice C — ‘examines’: A finite present-tense verb. Same problem as A — two main verbs. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘examined’: A finite past-tense verb. Same problem as A and C. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
